mission.title: Install and Configure Containerssh on Kubernetes
mission.description: ContainerSSH is an SSH server that launches containers on demand in Kubernetes and Docker, providing dynamic access for various use cases such as lab environments, debugging, and honeypots. Installing ContainerSSH allows you to manage containerized environments efficiently and securely.

steps.title: Install ContainerSSH via Helm
steps.description:
Add the ContainerSSH Helm chart and install:
```bash
helm repo add containerssh https://containerssh.github.io/containerssh
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace containerssh
helm install containerssh containerssh/containerssh --namespace containerssh
```
Alternatively, use the Docker image:
```bash
docker run -d -p 2222:2222 containerssh/containerssh:0.5.0
```

steps.title: Configure the authentication webhook
steps.description:
ContainerSSH requires an authentication webhook. Create a simple auth server or use the test server:
```bash
docker run -d -p 8080:8080 containerssh/containerssh-test-authconfig-server:0.5.0
```

steps.title: Test SSH access
steps.description:
Connect via SSH to the ContainerSSH server:
```bash
ssh -p 2222 user@localhost
```
Each SSH session launches a new container.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerssh
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerssh -n containerssh
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Helm release
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall containerssh -n containerssh
kubectl delete namespace containerssh
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade containerssh containerssh/containerssh --namespace containerssh
```

troubleshooting.title: SSH connection refused
troubleshooting.description:
Check that the ContainerSSH pod is running and the service is exposed:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n containerssh
kubectl get pods -n containerssh
```

troubleshooting.title: Authentication webhook unreachable
troubleshooting.description: Verify the auth server URL in the ContainerSSH config and check connectivity from the pod.

troubleshooting.title: Container launch failures
troubleshooting.description:
Check ContainerSSH logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerssh -n containerssh
```

resolution.summary: ContainerSSH is installed and provides SSH access that launches isolated containers for each session.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a working Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
